_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize AK House delegation batch: Anchorage districts 14-18 (Galvin, Costello, Hall, Fields, Nelson). State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence. Nelson currency fix: announced 2025-11-18 he is NOT running 2026 (lame duck).

profile.background: Independent (formerly Democrat) Rep., AK House District 14 (Anchorage: Spenard/Midtown/Rogers Park). Sits in the Democrat-led bipartisan House Majority Coalition; on House Finance. Co-founder of Great Alaska Schools (public-ed funding advocacy). Ran for US House vs. Don Young 2018/2020.

alyse-galvin.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Reliably progressive social/fiscal record: Planned Parenthood + EMILY's List-backed (PP funding documented), prime sponsor of statewide income tax HB152, sponsor of SOGI nondiscrimination bill HB301, voted NO on the trans-sports ban (HB183), opposed gender-dysphoria-treatment budget cut, favors reduced PFD with new revenue. Cross-party deviations: explicit pro-Second-Amendment/pro-hunting framing (tempered by support for expanded background checks) and support for funding police/Troopers (no defund). RCV, immigration, 10th-Amendment, most parental-rights positions: no public evidence found. Freedom Index 11%; iVoterGuide 'Liberal'. 2026 reelection filing not yet confirmed in sources (term ends Jan 2027); sitting incumbent.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood and EMILY's List; PP Federation contributions documented (iVoterGuide).

1.note: Sponsors HB301 adding sexual orientation / gender identity as protected classes.

2.note: Voted NO on the trans-sports ban (HB183); has a transgender daughter; opposed gender-dysphoria-treatment cut.

4.note: Prime sponsor of SOGI nondiscrimination bill HB301; opposed budget cut to gender-dysphoria treatment.

0.note: Campaign platform: fund understaffed Troopers/VPSOs and community-policing grants to hire officers; no defund rhetoric.

profile.background: Republican Rep., AK House District 15 (Anchorage). Veteran legislator: former Senate Majority Leader; moved Senate->House in 2025. Led the GOP House minority caucus Jan-Nov 2025. Energy/economy-focused (Cook Inlet gas, Alaska LNG).

mia-costello.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Documented record is economically conservative/anti-tax (spending-cap advocate, PFD protection, NO on pension reopening HB78 and childcare tax incentives SB96), firmly pro-2A (opposed red-flag HB89 as a 2nd-Amendment violation), pro-life (lead co-sponsor of the 2010 parental-notification initiative, voted YES on the Medicaid-abortion-ban amendment SA13, Alaska Family Council endorsement), and tough-on-crime (co-sponsored SB91 in 2016 then reversed and led its repeal). Voted YES on SA3 to defund CRT in schools; states school choice as a caucus priority; SA11 anti-COVID-mandate vote. Moderate 50% lifetime Freedom Index. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/LGBTQ, immigration, ranked-choice voting, or most election-integrity items (prior uncited TRUEs cleared to null). Sitting incumbent; no 2026 filing confirmed.

4.note: Pro-life record: lead co-sponsor of 2010 parental-notification initiative, voted YES on Medicaid-abortion-ban amendment SA13, Alaska Family Council endorsement; no abortion-industry funding found.

0.note: Set school choice as a stated GOP minority-caucus priority as caucus leader.

2.note: Voted YES on SA3, the amendment to defund Critical Race Theory in public schools.
Advocates a constitutional spending cap + sustainable fiscal plan; anti-tax; voted NO on reopening defined-benefit pensions (HB78) and on childcare tax incentives (SB96).

0.note: 'Led the charge to toughen criminal penalties' and 'end criminal catch-and-release'; pro-law-enforcement posture.

1.note: Reversed her early SB91 (soft-on-crime reform) support and introduced a repeal bill, saying it gave criminals 'a green light'.
Voted YES on SA11, barring state funds to entities mandating COVID-19 vaccines (federal-mandate resistance).
Opposed red-flag bill HB89: 'this law violates the Second Amendment... opens the door to government overreach and abuse.'

profile.background: First-term Democrat Rep., AK House District 16 (Anchorage: Turnagain/Spenard/Sand Lake). Sits in the bipartisan House Majority Coalition; co-chaired Labor & Commerce. Won 2024 with 56.6%. Reportedly running for reelection in 2026.

carolyn-hall.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Short but consistently progressive record: prime sponsor of a Medicaid family-planning expansion (HB185), cosponsor of SOGI nondiscrimination (HB301), public-education funding (HB69/28/231), labor and paid-leave bills (HB193/302) and an income-tax vehicle (HB350), and campaign-finance limits (HB16); favors keeping Alaska's open primaries / ranked-choice voting. No pro-life or pro-gun crossover found. Genuinely thin on guns, immigration, federalism, and Christian-liberty (NO EVIDENCE FOUND). Planned Parenthood endorsement could not be confirmed by name (source blocked).

1.note: Cosponsor of HB301 prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation / gender identity or expression.

4.note: Cosponsored SOGI nondiscrimination bill HB301; campaign favors LGBTQ anti-discrimination.

profile.background: Multi-term labor Democrat, AK House District 17 (downtown Anchorage; previously District 20). Co-Chair of House Labor & Commerce and House Majority Whip in the bipartisan House Majority Coalition. Won 2024 with 93.6%. ACU-AK rating ~9%/22%.

zack-fields.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Progressive labor record: pro-abortion-rights (Planned Parenthood-endorsed; said the AK privacy right covers abortion and it is 'unconscionable to take away access'), pro-LGBTQ (voted NO on the trans-sports ban HB183, cosponsored LGBTQ nondiscrimination HB17), pro-gun-control (cosponsored red-flag HB62: 'I strongly support red flag laws'), pro-RCV, pro-new-revenue with a smaller PFD. Marquee 2026 act: an 'ICE OUT of Alaska' floor protest of federal immigration enforcement (Republicans' censure attempt failed). Cross-party deviation: a law-and-order emphasis ('expand law enforcement capacity', cosponsored violent-crime sentencing bill HB14) rare for his lane. No 2026 filing confirmed; sitting incumbent.

0.note: Says the AK constitutional privacy right includes abortion and it is 'unconscionable to take away women's access to health care.'

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates.

1.note: Cosponsor of statewide LGBTQ nondiscrimination bill HB17.

2.note: Voted NO on the HB183 trans-sports ban; joined the minority filibuster against it.

4.note: Cosponsored LGBTQ nondiscrimination bill HB17 (pro-LGBTQ policy promotion).

0.note: Stated #1 priority: 'fight crime with expanded law enforcement capacity'; no defund stance.

2.note: Co-sponsored HB14, a violent-crime/sentencing-enhancement bill (assault, strangulation, sex offenses).

1.note: Held an 'ICE OUT of Alaska' sign protesting federal immigration enforcement / deportations.

2.note: 'ICE OUT' protest opposing federal enforcement; pro-immigrant posture contrary to anti-sanctuary position.

1.note: Cosponsored red-flag bill HB62: 'I strongly support red flag laws so people who have a demonstrated risk of violence have less access to weapons.'

profile.background: Sitting Republican Rep., AK House District 18 (JBER/Muldoon/Government Hill, north Anchorage); in the 19-member GOP minority caucus. Won the seat back from Democrat Cliff Groh in 2024 (50.2%). Strong pro-military/veterans record (sponsored HB125 military-family hiring preference, HJR16). Moderate voting record (22% lifetime CPAC).

david-nelson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). CURRENCY: announced 2025-11-18 he will NOT seek reelection in 2026 (cited his August marriage); Democrat Cliff Groh has filed for the seat -> candidacy_status set to not_running (still seated until Jan 2027). One of the chamber's most moderate Republicans (22% CPAC). Documented cross-party deviations: voted YES on HB195 (May 2026, 32-8), a bill Alaska pro-life groups/ADF call a chemical-abortion expansion, and voted against the protective Allard amendment; voted to OVERRIDE Gov. Dunleavy's veto of ~$51M in public-school funding (HB57) despite the governor's accountability objections. On marriage, guns, elections/RCV, immigration, 10th Amendment, and justice there is NO documented record (left null, not inferred).

1.note: Voted YES on HB195 (32-8), which pro-life groups/ADF call a chemical-abortion expansion, and voted against the protective Allard amendment (failed 23-17).
